TT95|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.195 / physical PDF p.213 (TT95 Mery, First prophet of Amūn — USURPER of TT84). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Amūn` macron + drops `(See also usurpation in tomb 84.)` parenthetical + adds TT84 to shared_with_tombs; B=`Amun` no macron + same drop + same shared_with_tombs; C=`Amūn` macron + KEEPS parenthetical + shared_with_tombs=[]. Agent C is structurally correct on the cross-ref handling: PM's `(See also usurpation in tomb 84.)` is an EVENT cross-reference (usurpation), NOT an OWNERSHIP/sharing relation. The chunk-9-onward `shared_with_tombs` rule documents `See also Tomb N` and `Also owner of tomb N` patterns — both ownership-flavoured. `See also usurpation in tomb N` does not match either pattern. Pin a value combining: agent A's macron-Ū (PM body-prose verbatim policy, chunk-12-onward macron-retain) + agent C's parenthetical-preserved position. shared_with_tombs is corrected to [] via CHUNK18_CORRECTIONS (overrides the A+B 2/1 majority on the structurally-incorrect `["TT84"]` pin) — back-ref symmetry test (TT84→TT95) would fail otherwise. is_usurped is pinned to false via DERIVER_OVERRIDES (the regex would spuriously fire on `usurpation`; Mery is the USURPER of TT84, not the usurped party at TT95).
TT95|notes_from_pm.value: First prophet of Amūn. (See also usurpation in tomb 84.) Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 70, HAY, No. 14.) Parents, Nebpeḥtireʿ, First prophet of Min of Koptos, and Ḥunay(t), Chief nurse of the Lord of the Two Lands (name from tomb 84). Wife, Dey.

TT97|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.203 / physical PDF p.221 (TT97 Amenemḥet, First prophet of Amūn). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=`wʿab-priest` (ayin-before-a) + `Amūn` (macron) ×2, B=`waʿb-priest` (ayin-after-a) + `Amun` (no macron) ×2, C=`waʿb-priest` (ayin-after-a) + `Amūn` (macron) ×2. Pin agent A: (1) ayin-before-a `wʿab-priest` matches the TT14 / TT68 / TT79 chunk-12-and-16 ayin-position precedent (PM's text-layer `r̄` bar-a is canonically read as ayin BEFORE the `a`); (2) macron-Ū on `Amūn` matches PM body-prose verbatim policy (chunk-12-onward macron-retain). Note: father `Ḏḥutiḥotp` (d-bar Ḏ + ḥ-underdot) preserved verbatim — PM p.203 prints both diacritics on this Thoth-derived theophoric.
TT97|notes_from_pm.value: First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Father, Ḏḥutiḥotp, wʿab-priest, Overseer of sandal-makers of the Temple of Amūn.

TT100|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.206 / physical PDF p.224 (TT100 Rekhmireʿ, Governor of the town and Vizier). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=`wʿab-priest of Amūn` (ayin-before-a, macron-Ū), B=`waʿb-priest of Amun` (ayin-after-a, no macron), C=`waʿb-priest of Amūn` (ayin-after-a, macron-Ū). Pin agent A: (1) ayin-before-a `wʿab-priest` matches the TT14 / TT68 / TT79 chunk-12-and-16 ayin-position precedent (PM's text-layer `r̄` bar-a is canonically read as ayin BEFORE the `a`); (2) macron-Ū on `Amūn` matches PM body-prose verbatim policy (chunk-12-onward macron-retain). The capital macron on `REKHMIRĒʿ` headword (printed p.206) is NOT in this field — it's restored on `occupant_name` via CHUNK18_CORRECTIONS per direct PDF visual.
TT100|notes_from_pm.value: Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 58, WILKINSON, No. 35.) Parents, Neferweben, [Vizier], wʿab-priest of Amūn, and Bet. Wife, Meryt.

DAN-AhmosiHenutempet|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.604 (DAN-AhmosiHenutempet). Agents split 1/1/1: A=ʿAhhotp + Seqenenreʿ + Coffin clause, B=ʿAhhotp + Seqenenreʿ no Coffin clause, C=ʿAḥḥotp + Seḳenenreʿ diacritic-rich variant + Coffin clause. Pin agent A's longest form with the load-bearing `Coffin possibly from here` provenance hedge; the underdot diacritics on ʿAḥḥotp / Seḳenenreʿ are post-merge corrections layered by fix_rows.py per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy.
DAN-AhmosiHenutempet|notes_from_pm.value: Daughter of ʿAhhotp (wife of King Seqenenreʿ-Taʿa). Coffin possibly from here.

DAN-AhmosiNefertere|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.599 (DAN-AhmosiNefertere). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=bare attribution headline only, B=bare Carter/Winlock/Černý attribution chain (NO headline), C=full headline + abbreviated Carter chain. Pin agent A's bare headline — the Carter / Winlock / Černý attribution chain is extended post-merge by fix_rows.py DAN-AhmosiNefertere entry per PM p.599 printed prose.

DAN-AntefSekhemreHeruhirmaet|occupant_na.rationale: PM I.2 p.603 (DAN-AntefSekhemreHeruhirmaet). Agents split 1/1/1 on `occupant_name`: A=`Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Heruhirmaʿet)`, B=`Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Heruḥirmaʿet)` (with ḥ underdot variant), C=`Antef` (bare, no parenthetical — agent dropped the disambiguator). Pin agent A's verbatim form with the parenthetical preserved; the Heruhirmaʿet → Heruḥirmaʿet diacritic correction is layered by fix_rows.py per the PM underdot policy. Bare `Antef` is wrong — without the parenthetical Sekhemreʿ-Heruhirmaʿet, this row collides with multiple other Antefs in PM's Dyn XVII clusters.

DAN-Aqhor|occupant_name.rationale: PM I.2 p.605 (DAN-Aqhor; PM headword printed as `ʿAḲ-ḤOR`). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`ʿAq-hor` (no underdot, lowercase 'h'), B=`ʿAq-Hor` (no underdot, capital 'H'), C=`ʿAḳ-hor` (underdot ḳ). Pin agent A's `ʿAq-hor` — the underdot ḳ correction (matching PM headword `ʿAḲ-ḤOR`) is layered by fix_rows.py DAN-Aqhor entry, which also handles the tomb_id rename from the historical `DAN-Ahhor` form to the canonical `DAN-Aqhor`. Override matches pre-fix-rows merge output that fix_rows expects to mutate.

DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.604 (DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Djhuti`, B=`Djehuty` (vowel-extended Anglicised form), C=`Djḥuti` (underdot ḥ variant). Pin agent A's `Djhuti` matching the canonical PM tomb_id form (CHUNK7_RENAMES: `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjehuti` → `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti`). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.604 actually prints sentence-form `QUEEN MENTUḤOTP¹, wife of King Ḏḥuti` (the `¹` is footnote anchor, NOT numeral I) — the consonant in `Ḏḥuti` is `Ḏ` (d with bar/macron, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character as in `Ḏḥwty`/Thoth), NOT `Ḍ` (d with underdot, used in some Semitic transliteration systems). They are different consonants representing different sounds. Wrong-consonant risk: a row keyed on `Ḍḥuti` would never match against TLA / Trismegistos / museum data using `Ḏḥuti`. The underdot/d-bar correction `Djhuti` → `Ḏḥuti` is layered by fix_rows.py per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy.

KV36|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.556 (KV36 Mahirper). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=longest with `Excavated by Loret`, B=truncated drops the Loret clause, C=truncated + Ḥatshepsut diacritic variant. Pin agent A's longest verbatim form (matches PM p.556 printed completeness). The Hatshepsut → Ḥatshepsut diacritic correction is applied post-merge by fix_rows.py KV36 entry per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy; ties are resolved at merge time, diacritics at fix_rows time.
KV36|notes_from_pm.value: Standard-bearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Hatshepsut. Excavated by Loret.

KV39|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.559 (KV39). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`. Even agent A's longest form was a TRUNCATION — PM p.559 prints the full paragraph through `... infra, p. 599.` (egyptologist verified printed source PR #151 review). Pinned value extended to PM-verbatim form: includes the load-bearing Abbott Papyrus + Peet citation chain (independent reason to doubt the Amenophis I attribution) AND the `infra, p. 599` cross-reference linking KV39 to DAN-AhmosiNefertere. The `Ḥ` underdot on `ʿAhmosi`/`ʿAḥmosi` is layered post-merge by fix_rows.py per the underdot policy.
KV39|notes_from_pm.value: Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen ʿAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.

KV42|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.559 (KV42 Tuthmosis II). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Attribution uncertain (?).` (with terminal period), B=null (agent dropped the uncertainty marker), C=`Attribution uncertain (?)` (no terminal period). Pin agent A's value with terminal period — fix_rows.py KV42 entry then rewrites to `(?). Excavated by Loret` per PM p.559 prints `42. TUTHMOSIS II (?)` headword convention; the override keeps merge output stable, fix_rows handles the canonical rewrite.

QV47|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.755 (QV47 ʿAhmosi). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=Sit-ḍḥout no Bibl (WRONG-CONSONANT — `ḍ` d-underdot, used in Semitic transliteration), B=Sit-ḍḥout with `(Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)` (same wrong consonant), C=Sit-ḏḥout with Bibl (CORRECT consonant `ḏ` d-bar, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character used for the deity Ḏḥuti/Thoth). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.755 prints `Sit-ḏḥout` with `ḏ` (d-bar), NOT `ḍ` (d-underdot). The value pinned here uses the egyptologically-correct consonant — agents A and B were wrong, agent C was right. The bare-form-without-Bibl is intentional: fix_rows.py QV47 entry restores the Bibl. ribbon post-merge per the egyptologist-reviewer's printed-source restoration. The `Ē` macron on `Seḳenenrēʿ` is layered by fix_rows.py per the diacritic policy.
QV47|notes_from_pm.value: daughter of Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and Sit-ḏḥout. Dyn. XVII.

QV74|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.767 (QV74 Tentopet). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm` because they all attempted to synthesize main-text + footnote 1 content into a single notes blob. Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 surfaced this as a rule-1 (provenance) violation: PM p.767 main text reads `74. QUEEN TENTŌPET [cartouche], Great King's mother and King's wife.¹ (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)` and footnote 1 carries the Wife/mother/daughter-of-Ramesses-IV genealogy with three independent citations (Gauthier / Černý / Seele). Pinning a synthesized form put non-PM-verbatim prose into `notes_from_pm`. Fixed: pin PM main-text VERBATIM only. The footnote 1 genealogy + per-citation chain is dropped from `notes_from_pm` here; the schema currently has no `notes_footnote` / `notes_genealogy` field, so the genealogy is tracked for restoration via follow-up issue (schema split). The `Ō` macron on `TENTŌPET` headword convention is recorded in `occupant_name`, out of scope for this notes override.
QV74|notes_from_pm.value: Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)

SWV-HatshepsutSouth|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.591 (SWV-HatshepsutSouth). Agents split 1/1/1: A=bare cross-ref + bare Sarcophagus mention, B=adds `South Tomb of Ḥatshepsut.` headline + `quartzite` material, C=A's structure + `quartzite` material. Pin agent A's bare form — the `South Tomb of Ḥatshepsut.` headline is generated downstream from `valley` + `tomb_id`, and the `quartzite` material detail is layered by fix_rows.py SWV-HatshepsutSouth entry (PM p.591 prints quartzite + Cairo Mus. Ent. number that fix_rows expands).
SWV-HatshepsutSouth|notes_from_pm.value: See also Tomb 20, supra, p. 546. Sarcophagus as Queen-Consort.

TT12|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.24 / physical PDF p.42 (TT12 Hray). Agents split 1/1/1 on two differences: (1) capitalisation of CHAMPOLLION — PM prints small caps throughout, rendering as ALL-CAPS; agent A emitted lowercase `Champollion` (wrong). (2) punctuation after the bibliographic ribbon close-paren — PM prints `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 51, L. D. Text, No. 2.) Mother,` with no period after the closing paren before `Mother`; agent B added a spurious `.` producing `.).` (wrong). Agent C matches PM verbatim on both points: ALL-CAPS CHAMPOLLION, no double-period. Pinning agent C's value. The `ʿAḥmosi` diacritic in the Mother clause is verbatim-preserve (notes_from_pm field) — no fix_rows correction needed.
TT12|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of the granary of the King's wife and King's mother ʿAḥḥotp. Temp. Amosis to Amenophis I (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 51, L. D. Text, No. 2.) Mother, ʿAḥmosi.

TT14|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.26 / physical PDF p.44 (TT14 Huy). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`wʿab-priest` (lowercase w, ayin ʿ present), B=`wab-priest` (lowercase w, ayin dropped), C=`Wʿab-priest` (uppercase W, ayin present). PM headword prints `war̄b-priest` — the bar-a is the PM text-layer rendering of the ayin (ʿ), and the word is lowercase following the tomb-number opener. Agent A matches on both points. Agents B dropped the ayin; agent C capitalised incorrectly.
TT14|notes_from_pm.value: wʿab-priest of 'Amenophis, the favourite of Amūn'. Ramesside.

TT26|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.43 / physical PDF p.61 (TT26 Khnememhab). Agents split 1/1/1: A=Amūn (macron correct) + spurious extra period `.). Wife` (double-period wrong); B=Amūn (macron correct) + `.) Wife` (no extra period, correct punctuation); C=Amun (macron stripped, wrong) + `.) Wife`. PM p.43 prints `Amūn` with macron throughout. The `(L. D. Text, No. 29.)` parenthetical closes with its own period — no second period follows before `Wife`. Pin agent B's form: Amūn with macron, no extra period after close-paren.
TT26|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of the treasury in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 29.) Wife, Meryesi.

TT29|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.45 / physical PDF p.63 (TT29 Amenemopet/Pairi). Agents split 1/1/1: A=[Caḥmosi] + extra period .). Parents (both wrong); B=[Aḥmosi] + no extra period (best candidate; minority — agents A and C both produced [Caḥmosi], mis-reading the ayin as a leading C; agent B alone got the bracket + name-root + punctuation right, only the leading ayin missing); C=[Caḥmosi] + no extra period. Pin agent B per the project principle that ties are resolved at merge time, diacritics at fix_rows time (KV36 precedent). The ayin restoration to PM-faithful [ʿAḥmosi] is layered post-merge by CHUNK11_CORRECTIONS for TT29 notes_from_pm. Per Gemini Code Assist PR #199 round 1 (separation-of-concerns refactor) and round 2 + round 3 (majority/minority wording correction + punctuation cleanup); the prior single-step override was architecturally inconsistent.
TT29|notes_from_pm.value: Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (HAY, No. 15.) Parents, [Aḥmosi] Ḥumay (tomb 224) and Nub. Wife, Wertmaʿetef.

TT4|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.11 (TT4 Ḳen). Agents split 1/1/1 on text-layer-noise variants for the parent name (`Thoniifer` vs `Thonufer`) and on whether to add a redundant trailing period after the bibliographic close-paren. Pin a stable post-merge form so the merge step has a deterministic answer for the tie; egyptologist printed-source corrections (macron restoration on `Amūn` ×2 + `Thonūfer`, plus the medial-underdot drop on `Ḥenutmeḥyt` → `Ḥenutmehyt`) are layered post-merge by `CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS` in `fix_rows.py`. The pinned-merge value here corresponds to the chunk-9 PR's first-pass merge output before the egyptologist's printed-source review; the final on-disk value comes from CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS, which the per-row test asserts. Keeping merge-time and fix_rows-time overrides separate maintains the audit-trail boundary (merge resolves agent ties; fix_rows applies reviewer-cited PM-faithful corrections).
TT4|notes_from_pm.value: Chiseller of Amun in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 106.) Parents, Thonufer, Chiseller of Amun in the Khenu, and Maʿetnefert. Wives, Nefertere and Ḥenutmeḥyt.

TT31|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.47 / physical PDF p.65 (TT31 Khons). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(L. D. Text, No. 51.). Parents,` (period inside and outside the close-paren); B=clean `(L. D. Text, No. 51.) Parents,` (no extra period); C=citation moved to sentence-end `... Wives, Ruia and Mutia or May. (L. D. Text, No. 51.)`. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence, closing with a single period inside the paren — no second period follows before `Parents`. Same pattern as TT26 (p.43) where agent B's clean form was also pinned. Pin agent B.
TT31|notes_from_pm.value: First prophet of Menkheperreʿ (Tuthmosis III). Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 51.) Parents, Neferḥotep, First prophet of Amenophis II, and Tausert, Songstress of Monthu. Wives, Ruia and Mutia or May.

TT32|occupant_name.rationale: PM I.1 p.49 / physical PDF p.67 (TT32). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Thutmosi` (no diacritic); B=`Ḏhutmosi` (d-bar Ḏ — egyptologically correct, the standard transliteration of the d-emphatic in `Ḏḥwty`/Thoth and its derived names); C=`Hutmosi` (prefix consonant dropped). pypdf text-layer renders `{>l;IUTMOSI` (the OCR signature for capital d-emphatic + capital underdot-Ḥ). Pin agent A's `Thutmosi` as the PDF-closest stripped-diacritic form so the merge step has a deterministic answer; CHUNK12_CORRECTIONS in fix_rows.py layers the post-merge restoration to `Ḏhutmosi` (d-bar) per the PR #151 precedent (egyptologist verified `Ḏ` not `Ḍ` for `Ḏḥuti`/`Sit-ḏḥout` after direct PDF read of PM I.2 p.604 and p.755). The d-bar is the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic; the underdot is a different consonant used in some Semitic systems and would never match TLA/Trismegistos/museum data using `Ḏḥwty`-derived forms.

TT33|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.49 / physical PDF p.67 (TT33 Pedamenopet). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(L. D. Text, No. 20.). Mother,`; B=clean `(L. D. Text, No. 20.) Mother,`; C=citation moved to sentence-end. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence with a single period — same pattern as TT26 (p.43) and TT31 (p.47). Pin agent B.
TT33|notes_from_pm.value: Prophet, Chief lector. Saite. (L. D. Text, No. 20.) Mother, Namenkhesi, Sistrum-player of Amūn. Wife, Tedi.

TT35|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.61 / physical PDF p.79 (TT35 Bekenkhons). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 45, L. D. Text, Nos. 10, 11.). Parents,`; B=clean `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 45, L. D. Text, Nos. 10, 11.) Parents,`; C=citation moved to sentence-end. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence — no second period after the close-paren before `Parents`. Same pattern as TT26/TT31/TT33. Pin agent B.
TT35|notes_from_pm.value: First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 45, L. D. Text, Nos. 10, 11.) Parents, Roma, First and second prophet of Amūn, and Roma, Singer of Amūn. Wife, Mertesger, Chief of the harim of Amūn.

TT36|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.63 / physical PDF p.81 (TT36 Ibi). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 56, L. D. Text, No. 25.). Parents,`; B=clean `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 56, L. D. Text, No. 25.) Parents,`; C=citation moved to sentence-end. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence — same pattern as TT26/TT31/TT33/TT35. Pin agent B.

TT37|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.68 / physical PDF p.86 (TT37 Harua). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 54, L. D. Text, No. 23.). Parents,`; B=clean `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 54, L. D. Text, No. 23.) Parents,`; C=citation moved to sentence-end. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence — same pattern as TT26/TT31/TT33/TT35/TT36. Pin agent B.
TT37|notes_from_pm.value: Chief steward of the god's wife Amenardais I. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 54, L. D. Text, No. 23.) Parents, Pedemut, Scribe, and Estawert (names from statues, in Berlin Mus. 8163, see infra, p. 69, and Cairo Mus. Ent. 36711).

TT39|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.71 / physical PDF p.89 (TT39 Puimreʿ). Agents split 1/1/1: A=spurious double-period `(L. D. Text, No. 18.). Parents,`; B=clean `(L. D. Text, No. 18.) Parents,`; C=citation moved to sentence-end. PM prints the bibliographic parenthetical mid-sentence — same pattern as TT26/TT31/TT33/TT35/TT36/TT37. Pin agent B.
TT39|notes_from_pm.value: Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 18.) Parents, Puia and Neferi. Wives, Tanefert and Sensonb.

TT40|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.75 / physical PDF p.93 (TT40 Amenhotp, called Ḥuy). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`called Ḥuy, Viceroy...` + spurious double-period `(CHAMPOLLION, A, L. D. Text, No. 110.). Mother,`; B=`Viceroy...` + clean `(CHAMPOLLION, A, L. D. Text, No. 110.) Mother,`; C=`Viceroy...` + citation moved to sentence-end. Content split on `called Ḥuy,`: agents B and C agree the epithet does not belong in `notes_from_pm` — it is already encoded in `occupant_alt_names: ["Huy"]`. PM's headword construction `AMENḤOTP, called HUY` places the epithet as part of the name entry; the alt_names field captures it. Dropping it from notes avoids duplication. Pin agent B (content correct, punctuation correct — same TT26 precedent as the other six chunk-12 notes ties).
TT40|notes_from_pm.value: Viceroy of Kush, Governor of the South Lands. Temp. Amenophis IV to Tutʿankhamūn. (CHAMPOLLION, A, L. D. Text, No. 110.) Mother, Wenḥo.

TT53|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.102 / physical PDF p.120 (TT53 Amenemhet). Agents split 1/1/1: A=macron-preserving `Yotefnūfer`/`Tetiemnūter` + clean punctuation (no double-period); B=no macrons `Yotefnufer`/`Tetiemnuter` + clean punctuation; C=no macrons + spurious double-period `(CHAMPOLLION, No. 16 bis, L. D. Text, No. 78.).`. Per the verbatim-preserve policy for `notes_from_pm` (PM prints vowel macrons in body prose — same policy that drove the TT26 / TT31 / TT33 / TT35 / TT36 / TT37 / TT39 / TT40 series) and the `Amūn` macron-retention precedent on TT26 (p.43), pin agent A: macrons on `Yotefnūfer`/`Tetiemnūter` match PM's body-prose diacritic style, and the clean (no double-period) punctuation is correct.

Pin candidate 3 (agent C's form): mid-sentence citation (consistent with PM's convention and the chunk-12 cluster TT26/TT31/TT33/TT35/TT36/TT37/TT39/TT40) + `Ḥepu` with underdot-ḥ matching the printed source. Agent A's `Hepu` (no diacritic) contradicts the source. Agent B's end-sentence citation contradicts PM's page structure.
TT107|notes_from_pm.value: Royal scribe, Steward of the estate of Amenophis III 'Reʿ is brilliant'. Temp. Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 33, L. D. Text, No. 37.) Parents, Neby, Judge, and Ḥepu.

TT110|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.227 / physical PDF p.245 (TT110 Ḏhout). Agents split 1/1/1 on the wife's name: A=`Baktḥ.` (d-bar family name with underdot-ḥ); B=`Baktḥ[or].` (added `[or]` lacuna bracket — not in source); C=`Bakt.` (stripped underdot-ḥ). Direct reading of PM I.1 p.227 / PDF p.245 confirms `Wife, Baktḥ.` — the name ends with ḥ followed by a hieroglyphic determinative glyph and a period; there is no lacuna and no `[or]` bracket in the printed text. Agent B's `[or]` is an OCR mis-reading of the hieroglyphic determinative cluster that follows the name. Agent C stripped the ḥ diacritic. Pin agent A: `Baktḥ.` matches the printed source exactly.
TT110|notes_from_pm.value: Royal butler, Royal herald. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III. Parents, Pesediri (?) and Keku. Wife, Baktḥ.

TT112|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.229 / physical PDF p.247 (TT112 Menkheperraʿsonb, usurped by ʿAshefytemweset). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Amūn` (macron) + `Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset)` (source-faithful order); B=`Amun` (no macron) + `Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset)` (source-faithful order); C=`Amūn` (macron) + `Wife (of ʿAshefytemweset)` (parenthetical transposed). PM chunk-20 source text p.247 prints `Wife of (<Ashefytemweset), Mutemwia` — the `of` precedes the parenthetical, not inside it. Agents A+B match the source-faithful parenthetical order; agent C transposed the `of` inside the paren. Pin agent A: (1) macron-Ū on `Amūn` matches PM body-prose verbatim policy (chunk-12-onward macron-retain precedent); (2) `Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset)` matches PM's printed parenthetical order. Agent B's no-macron form is wrong on axis 1; agent C's transposed parenthetical is wrong on axis 2.
TT112|notes_from_pm.value: Temp. Tuthmosis III. Usurped by ʿAshefytemweset, Prophet of Amūn 'Great of Majesty', Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 59.) Father (of ʿAshefytemweset), Pentawer. Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset), Mutemwia.

TT113|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.230 / physical PDF p.248 (TT113 Kynebu, wʿab-priest). Agents split 1/1/1 on three axes: (1) ayin-position — A=`Wʿab-priest` (capital W, ayin before a); B=`Waʿb-priest` (ayin transposed after a); C=`wʿb-priest` (lowercase w, no `a` between ayin and b — OCR noise). Per the TT14/TT68/TT97 ayin-before-a precedent, `wʿab` is PM-faithful. (2) father name — A=`Bekenamūn` (drops `t` consonant — wrong); B=`Bekenamtun` (consonant correct, no macron-Ū); C=`Bekenamtūn` (consonant correct + macron-Ū). PM source p.248 prints `Bekenamtln` (OCR ligature rendering of `Bekenamtūn` with macron-ū); `Bekenamūn` (agent A) drops the `t` — a consonant drop, not a diacritic variant. (3) secondary `wʿab`/`waʿb` on father's title — same ayin-before-a axis. Pin a value combining: ayin-before-a `wʿab` (correct per TT14/TT68/TT97 precedent) + lowercase initial (body prose, not heading) + `Bekenamtūn` (B's consonant-complete form with C's macron-Ū restoration, matching PM's `tln` → `tūn` OCR rendering). No single agent has all three axes correct; the pinned value takes the correct element on each axis with verbatim-preserve priority per rule 1. Note: `Wʿab-priest` capital-W in agent A is a heading-artefact (PM headword small-caps rendering) — body prose is lowercase.
TT113|notes_from_pm.value: wʿab-priest over-the-secrets of the estate of Amūn, Prophet in the Temple of Tuthmosis IV. Temp. Ramesses VIII. (WILKINSON, No. 2, HAY, No. 10.) Father, Bekenamtūn, wʿab-priest of Amūn. Wife, Esi.

TT114|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.231 / physical PDF p.249 (TT114 anonymous, Head of goldworkers). Agents split 1/1/1 on two axes: (1) deity epithet — A+C=`Amūn` (macron); B=`Amun` (no macron). (2) father's title — A=`wʿab-priest` (ayin before a, correct); B=`waʿb-priest` (ayin transposed); C=`wʿb-priest` (ayin before b, no intervening `a` — OCR noise). PM source p.249 prints `warb-priest of Anubis` (bar-a = ayin before a), confirming the `wʿab` form per TT14/TT68/TT97/TT113 ayin-before-a precedent. Pin agent A: (1) macron-Ū on `Amūn` matches PM body-prose verbatim policy; (2) `wʿab-priest` matches the ayin-before-a PM-faithful form. Note: occupant_role=Unknown (all 3 agents agree) is flagged for egyptologist review — PM's headword prints a title (`Head of goldworkers`) without a personal name, which may warrant role=Official; see egyptologist-pending note.
TT114|notes_from_pm.value: Head of goldworkers of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XX. Father, a wʿab-priest of Anubis.

TT120|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.234 / physical PDF p.252 (TT120 ʿAnen, Second prophet of Amūn). Agents split 1/1/1 on two axes: (1) deity macron — A+C=`Amūn` (macron); B=`Amun` (no macron). (2) alt-name diacritic — A=`Mahu` (no underdot-ḥ); B=`Maḥu` (underdot-ḥ, correct per PM's `MAI;IU` headword glyph); C=`Maḥu` (underdot-ḥ). PM chunk-20 source text p.252 prints `Called MAI;IU ~~ in GARDINER and WEIGALL, Cat.` — the `I;I` is the PM text-layer rendering of underdot-Ḥ (same glyph pattern as `MAḤU` in TT57 tie-break-override, where B+C had `Maḥu` and the underdot was confirmed by the TT57 `KHAʿEMḤET, called MAḤU` headword). Agent C has both correct axes: `Amūn` macron + `Maḥu` underdot-ḥ. Pin agent C. Note: `occupant_alt_names=["Mahu"]` (without underdot) wins via A+C 2/1 majority in the structured field — the underdot issue in notes is a verbatim-preserve question; the occupant_alt_names field strips diacritics per the matchable-name strip-ḥ rule and the TT57 `Mahu` precedent.
TT120|notes_from_pm.value: Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Parents, Yuia and Thuiu (tomb 46 in the Valley of the Kings). Called Maḥu in GARDINER and WEIGALL, Cat.

Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Overseers of the magazine of Amūn...` (drops the `with Chapels of Amenemḥet, both` clause — structurally incomplete); B=`with Chapels of Amenemḥet, both Overseers of the magazine of Amūn...` (PM-verbatim minus the redundant occupant-name prefix — structurally correct); C=`[Amen]ḥotp, with Chapels of Amenemḥet, both Overseers of the magazine of Amūn...` (prefixes the occupant_name field value, violating the project-wide no-duplicate-occupant-in-notes convention established across chunks 9-20).
